findings.title: Collections album covers are gradient placeholders for any trip not opened this session (photos never prefetched for the list)
findings.rootCause: tripCover() (helpers.ts:163-170) falls back coverUrl → trip.photos[0] → day.photos[0]. But /api/data deliberately ships the four heavy media fields empty; api.ts:178 cold-defaults trip.photos=[] for any trip not yet hydrated. fetchTripMedia() is only ever called for the active trip (api.ts:317), on trip-switch (bootstrap/trip-controls.ts:75), and when opening an archived-trip detail (collections.ts:56). It is NEVER called for the Collections list/overview render, so trip.photos is empty there for un-opened trips. Additionally, photos are written to the TRIP-level trip.photos[] array (addTripPhoto with a dayId), not the legacy trip_days.photos column, so tripCover()'s day.photos[0] fallback (helpers.ts:166-167) is also empty for modern trips. Template-instantiated trips have no coverUrl by design (src/routes/templates.py:257-258), so they are always gradient.

findings.title: ArchivedTripDetail shows owner-only Restore + privacy controls on foreign/active trips (dead button + select that persists nothing)
findings.rootCause: ArchivedTripDetail.tsx has no ownership gate — it always renders the Restore button (line 272) and the privacy select (lines 293-303). For foreign trips fetched via /api/public-trip (collections.ts:42 slow path) and for the user's own active trips (fast path), restoreTrip() (handlers.ts:60-62) and toggleTripPrivacy() (handlers.ts:36-37) both look the trip up in STATE.archivedTrips/STATE.trips and early-return when not found, so they no-op. The privacy <select> is uncontrolled (defaultValue), so its displayed value changes on the user's pick even though nothing persisted. The public-trip endpoint already returns ownerId on the trip (src/routes/public.py:185), so an ownership check is feasible.

findings.title: ArchivedCard list/drill-in cards render only coverUrl, never the photo fallback — inconsistent with the album overview and detail hero
findings.rootCause: ArchivedCard only renders trip.coverUrl (Collections.tsx:494 `{trip.coverUrl && }`) with no fallback. The album overview uses tripCover() (helpers.ts:163, which adds the trip.photos / day.photos fallback chain) and the detail hero uses the same chain (ArchivedTripDetail.tsx:91-99). So the card list is the odd one out — it ignores trip.photos entirely.

findings.title: Year-album shelf is not in chronological order under the default 'Recent' sort
findings.rootCause: groupTrips() (helpers.ts:187-207) orders albums by first appearance of each key in the already-sorted trip list. The default sort is 'recent' (state.ts:39), i.e. archivedAt-desc, so year-album order tracks the order in which each year's most-recently-archived trip appears — which is unrelated to the year value. Verified by simulation: trips archived in order across years 2021/2024/2021/2023 produce shelf order [2021, 2024, 2023].

findings.title: Hardcoded English strings in Collections / archived-detail surfaces (Sort/Filter titles, view-count, share tooltips)
findings.rootCause: These literals are inlined rather than passed through t(). It is also internally inconsistent: the neighbouring Group-by select correctly uses t('collections.groupByLabel') for its title/aria (Collections.tsx:288-289), while the Sort/year/destination selects right next to it are hardcoded.

findings.title: 'Most days' sort counts the day-0 Trip Hub while the card day count excludes it
findings.rootCause: The daysDesc sort uses (a.tripDays?.length || 0) (helpers.ts:131-135), which includes the day-0 Hub anchor. The card's displayed day count uses plannedDays = tripDays.filter(d => dayNumber > 0).length (Collections.tsx:480), explicitly excluding the Hub (per BUG-33). The two counts diverge whenever Hub rows differ (e.g. a duplicate day-0 that hasn't been self-healed yet).
